violations.text: Cheese was observed held at 49 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Product was disposed of. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026
The open air cooler was observed unable to hold TCS foods at adequate temperature. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

Cheese and eggs were observed held at 46-47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Items were stored overnight in unit. Product disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
The open air unit was observed unable to hold food at proper temperature. Unit must not be used for TCS foods until adjusted or repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

Raw animal foods (eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods in the open air cooler. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2024
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024

At the time of re-inspection, the open-air cooler was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 41 to 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Per the PIC, foods requiring refrigeration of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below are no longer stored in Open Air Cooler. Open Air Cooler is only used for Beverages/Food items that do not require Temperature Control of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/08/2023

At the time of inspection, several food items were observed at 48 to 53 degrees Fahrenheit in the Open Air Display Cooler (See Temperatures). Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

At the time of inspection, a chemical spray bottle was observed stored on top of milk in the walk-in cooler. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Chemical spray bottle was relocated at the time of isnepction. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

At the time of inspection, the Open Air Display Cooler in the retail area was not in good repair/properly adjusted. The cooler was observed to be maintaining an ambient air temperature of 51 degrees Fahrenheit with food temperatures maintained at 48 to 53 degrees Fahrenheit. The Open Air Display Cooler observed to be in poor repair/improperly adjusted is not to be used for storage of Time/Temperature Control for Safety Foods (Food items labeled KEEP REFRIGERATED) until unit is serviced/repaired. (Per the PIC, a call has been made for service previous day.) New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023
